server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acds_client_data_t
Per-client connection data. struct migration_context_t
In-memory host migration context. struct acds_stats_t
Network statistics for both TCP and WebSocket connections. struct acds_server_t
Discovery server state.

Functions


asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *server, const acds_config_t *config)
Initialize discovery server. void acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t *server)
Shutdown discovery server. void * acds_client_handler (void *arg)
Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). void * acds_websocket_client_handler (void *arg)
WebSocket client handler (thread entry point).

Function Documentation

void * acds_client_handler (void * arg)

Per-client connection handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP packets from a connected client. Handles crypto handshake, then dispatches packets to session/signaling handlers.

Parameters

arg Pointer to client connection context

Returns

NULL (thread exit value)

Definition at line 922 of file src/discovery-service/server.c.

922                                      {
923   tcp_client_context_t *ctx = (tcp_client_context_t *)arg;
924   if (!ctx) {
925     log_error("Client handler: NULL context");
926     return NULL;
927   }
928 
929   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
930   socket_t client_socket = ctx->client_socket;
931 
932   // Get client IP for logging
933   char client_ip[INET6_ADDRSTRLEN] = {0};
934   tcp_client_context_get_ip(ctx, client_ip, sizeof(client_ip));
935 
936   log_info("Client handler started for %s", client_ip);
937 
938   // Register client in TCP server registry with allocated client data
939   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
940   if (!client_data) {
941     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to allocate client data");
942     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
943     return NULL;
944   }
945   memset(client_data, 0, sizeof(*client_data));
946   client_data->joined_session = false;
947   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
948 
949   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
950   char crypto_name[96];
951   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_client_%s", client_ip);
952   asciichat_error_t handshake_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
953   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
954     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for client %s", client_ip);
955     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
956     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
957     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
958     return NULL;
959   }
960 
961   // Set server identity keys for handshake
962   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
963   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
964   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
965   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
966 
967   if (tcp_server_add_client(server->tcp_server, client_socket, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
968     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
969     tcp_server_reject_client(client_socket, "Failed to register client in registry");
970     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
971     return NULL;
972   }
973 
974   log_debug("Client %s registered (socket=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, client_socket,
975             tcp_server_get_client_count(server->tcp_server));
976 
977   // Create transport for handshake and subsequent client communication
978   acip_transport_t *transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("acds_tcp_client", client_socket, NULL);
979   if (!transport) {
980     log_error("Failed to create TCP transport for client %s", client_ip);
981     tcp_server_remove_client(server->tcp_server, client_socket);
982     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
983     return NULL;
984   }
985 
986   // Perform crypto handshake using shared server_like implementation
987   log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with client %s", client_ip);
988   handshake_result = session_server_like_handshake(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
989   if (handshake_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
990     log_warn("Crypto handshake failed for client %s", client_ip);
991     acip_transport_destroy(transport);
992     tcp_server_remove_client(server->tcp_server, client_socket);
993     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
994     return NULL;
995   }
996 
997   client_data->handshake_complete = true;
998   log_info("Crypto handshake complete for client %s", client_ip);
999 
1000   // Store transport and registry ID in client data, and link back from transport
1001   client_data->transport = transport;
1002   client_data->registry_id = client_socket;
1003   transport->user_data = client_data;
1004 
1005   // Main packet processing loop
1006   uint64_t last_packet_time_ns = time_get_ns();
1007   const uint64_t idle_disconnect_ns = 60ULL * NS_PER_SEC_INT; // Disconnect after 60s idle
1008 
1009   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server->running)) {
1010     packet_type_t packet_type;
1011     void *payload = NULL;
1012     size_t payload_size = 0;
1013 
1014     // Receive packet (blocking with system timeout)
1015     int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_size);
1016 
1017     if (result >= 0) {
1018       last_packet_time_ns = time_get_ns();
1019       log_info("★ ACDS_CLIENT_HANDLER: Received packet type=%u from %s, payload_size=%zu", packet_type, client_ip,
1020                payload_size);
1021 
1022       // Track network stats (TCP packets)
1023       // Packet size includes header (6 bytes: magic 4 + type 1 + length 2 + CRC 4)
1024       size_t total_packet_size = payload_size + 14; // 4 (magic) + 1 (type) + 2 (length) + 4 (CRC) + 3 (client ID)
1025       atomic_fetch_add_u64(&server->stats.bytes_received, total_packet_size);
1026       atomic_fetch_add_u64(&server->stats.packets_received, 1);
1027     }
1028 
1029     if (result < 0) {
1030       // Check error context to distinguish timeout from actual disconnect
1031       asciichat_error_context_t err_ctx;
1032       bool has_context = HAS_ERRNO(&err_ctx);
1033 
1034       // Check if this is a timeout (non-fatal) or actual disconnect (fatal)
1035       asciichat_error_t error = GET_ERRNO();
1036       if (error == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT ||
1037           (error == ERROR_NETWORK && has_context && strstr(err_ctx.context_message, "timed out") != NULL)) {
1038         // Check if client has been idle too long (abrupt disconnect without FIN)
1039         uint64_t idle_ns = time_get_ns() - last_packet_time_ns;
1040         if (idle_ns >= idle_disconnect_ns) {
1041           log_info("Client %s: idle for %llu seconds, disconnecting", client_ip,
1042                    (unsigned long long)(idle_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT));
1043           if (payload) {
1044             buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1045           }
1046           break;
1047         }
1048         log_debug("Client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1049         if (payload) {
1050           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1051         }
1052         continue;
1053       }
1054 
1055       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1056       log_info("Client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1057       if (payload) {
1058         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1059       }
1060       break;
1061     }
1062 
1063     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1064 
1065     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1066     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1067       bool allowed = (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING ||
1068                       packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1069 
1070       if (!allowed) {
1071         log_warn("Client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG "
1072                  "allowed",
1073                  client_ip, packet_type);
1074 
1075         // Send error response using persistent transport
1076         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1077                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1078 
1079         // Free payload and continue
1080         if (payload) {
1081           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1082         }
1083         continue;
1084       }
1085     }
1086 
1087     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1088     // Set server context for callbacks
1089     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1090     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1091 
1092     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1093         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1094 
1095     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1096       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1097                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1098     }
1099 
1100     // Free payload (allocated by receive_packet via buffer_pool_alloc)
1101     if (payload) {
1102       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_size);
1103     }
1104 
1105     // Keep handler running after session_established
1106     // Handler continues to receive and relay WebRTC signaling packets (SDP, ICE)
1107     // Broadcasts don't go through this socket - they use tcp_server_foreach_client() to write directly
1108   }
1109 
1110   // Cleanup - handler always cleans up since it runs until connection closes
1111   tcp_server_remove_client(server->tcp_server, client_socket);
1112   log_debug("Client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip, tcp_server_get_client_count(server->tcp_server));
1113 
1114   // Destroy transport before closing socket
1115   acip_transport_destroy(transport);
1116   socket_close(client_socket);
1117 
1118   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1119 
1120   log_info("Client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1121   return NULL;
1122 }

    acip_send_error(), acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,

    atomic_load_bool(), buffer_pool_free(),
    acds_stats_t::bytes_received,
    tcp_client_context_t::client_socket,
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,

    ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, GET_ERRNO,

    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx, HAS_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn, NS_PER_SEC_INT,

    PACKET_TYPE_PONG, acds_stats_t::packets_received,

    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key,
    session_server_like_handshake(), socket_close(),
    acds_server_t::stats, tcp_client_context_get_ip(),
    acds_server_t::tcp_server, tcp_server_add_client(),
    tcp_server_get_client_count(), tcp_server_reject_client(),
    tcp_server_remove_client(), time_get_ns(),

    public_key_t::type, acip_transport::user_data, and
    tcp_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().






asciichat_error_t acds_server_init (acds_server_t *
  server, const acds_config_t * config)


Initialize discovery server. Loads or generates identity keys,
    opens database, creates session registry, and binds TCP socket.




Parameters


server Server structure to initialize


Config Module Configuration from command-line
  parsing


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 194 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


194                                                                                        {
195   if (!server || !config) {
196     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "server or config is NULL");
197   }
198 
199   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
200   memcpy(&server->config, config, sizeof(acds_config_t));
201 
202   // Initialize stats
203   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.bytes_sent, 0);
204   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.bytes_received, 0);
205   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.packets_sent, 0);
206   atomic_store_u64(&server->stats.packets_received, 0);
207   server->stats.start_time = time(NULL);
208 
209   // Open database (SQLite as single source of truth)
210   asciichat_error_t result = database_init(config->database_path, &server->db);
211   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
212     return result;
213   }
214 
215   // Initialize rate limiter with SQLite backend
216   server->rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_sqlite(NULL); // NULL = externally managed DB
217   if (!server->rate_limiter) {
218     database_close(server->db);
219     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create rate limiter");
220   }
221 
222   // Set the database handle for the rate limiter
223   rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(server->rate_limiter, server->db);
224 
225   // Get TCP server from server_like (it owns the lifecycle)
226   server->tcp_server = session_server_like_get_tcp_server();
227   if (!server->tcp_server) {
228     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
229     database_close(server->db);
230     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "TCP server not available from server_like");
231   }
232 
233   // Initialize background worker thread pool
234   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, false);
235   server->worker_pool = thread_pool_create("acds_workers");
236   if (!server->worker_pool) {
237     log_warn("Failed to create worker thread pool");
238     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
239     database_close(server->db);
240     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create worker thread pool");
241   }
242 
243   // Spawn cleanup thread in worker pool
244   if (thread_pool_spawn(server->worker_pool, cleanup_thread_func, server, 0, "cleanup") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
245     log_warn("Failed to spawn cleanup thread (continuing without cleanup)");
246   }
247 
248   log_info("Discovery server initialized successfully");
249   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
250 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    atomic_store_u64(), acds_stats_t::bytes_received,
    acds_stats_t::bytes_sent, acds_server_t::config,
    database_close(), database_init(),
    acds_config_t::database_path, acds_server_t::db,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    log_info, log_warn, acds_stats_t::packets_received,
    acds_stats_t::packets_sent, acds_server_t::rate_limiter,
    rate_limiter_create_sqlite(), rate_limiter_destroy(),
    rate_limiter_set_sqlite_db(),
    session_server_like_get_tcp_server(), SET_ERRNO,
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_stats_t::start_time,
    acds_server_t::stats, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    thread_pool_create(), thread_pool_spawn(), and
    acds_server_t::worker_pool.






void
  acds_server_shutdown (acds_server_t * server)


Shutdown discovery server. Closes listen socket, stops accepting
    connections, waits for handler threads to exit, closes database, and frees
    resources.




Parameters


server Server structure to clean up


Definition at line 252 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


252                                                  {
253   if (!server) {
254     return;
255   }
256 
257   // Signal shutdown to worker threads
258   atomic_store_bool(&server->shutdown, true);
259 
260   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit
261   // TCP server is still alive (server_like destroys it after cleanup_fn)
262   if (server->tcp_server) {
263     size_t remaining_clients;
264     int shutdown_attempts = 0;
265     const int max_shutdown_attempts = 100; // 10 seconds (100 * 100ms)
266 
267     while ((remaining_clients = tcp_server_get_client_count(server->tcp_server)) > 0 &&
268            shutdown_attempts < max_shutdown_attempts) {
269       log_debug("Waiting for %zu client handler threads to exit (attempt %d/%d)", remaining_clients,
270                 shutdown_attempts + 1, max_shutdown_attempts);
271       APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
272       platform_sleep_ms(100);
273       shutdown_attempts++;
274     }
275 
276     if (remaining_clients > 0) {
277       log_warn("Server shutdown: %zu client handler threads still running after 10 seconds", remaining_clients);
278     } else if (shutdown_attempts > 0) {
279       log_debug("All client handler threads exited gracefully");
280     }
281   }
282 
283   // Stop and destroy worker thread pool (cleanup thread, etc.)
284   if (server->worker_pool) {
285     thread_pool_destroy(server->worker_pool);
286     server->worker_pool = NULL;
287     log_debug("Worker thread pool stopped");
288   }
289 
290   // Destroy rate limiter
291   if (server->rate_limiter) {
292     rate_limiter_destroy(server->rate_limiter);
293     server->rate_limiter = NULL;
294   }
295 
296   // Close database
297   if (server->db) {
298     database_close(server->db);
299     server->db = NULL;
300   }
301 
302   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
303 }






References APP_CALLBACK_VOID, atomic_store_bool(),
    database_close(), acds_server_t::db, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, platform_sleep_ms(),
    acds_server_t::rate_limiter, rate_limiter_destroy(),
    acds_server_t::shutdown, acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_get_client_count(), thread_pool_destroy(), and
    acds_server_t::worker_pool.






void
  * acds_websocket_client_handler (void * arg)


WebSocket client handler (thread entry point). Processes ACIP
    packets from a WebSocket client connection. Similar to TCP handler but uses
    transport-based APIs for crypto handshake.




Parameters


arg Pointer to
  websocket_client_context_t


Returns


NULL (thread exit value)


Definition at line 1128 of file
    src/discovery-service/server.c.


1128                                                {
1129   websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;
1130   if (!ctx) {
1131     log_error("WebSocket client handler: NULL context");
1132     return NULL;
1133   }
1134 
1135   acds_server_t *server = (acds_server_t *)ctx->user_data;
1136   acip_transport_t *transport = ctx->transport;
1137   char *client_ip = ctx->client_ip;
1138 
1139   log_info("WebSocket client handler started for %s", client_ip);
1140 
1141   // Allocate client_data
1142   acds_client_data_t *client_data = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acds_client_data_t), acds_client_data_t *);
1143   if (!client_data) {
1144     log_error("Failed to allocate client data for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1145     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1146     return NULL;
1147   }
1148 
1149   client_data->transport = transport;
1150   client_data->joined_session = false;
1151   client_data->handshake_complete = false;
1152   transport->user_data = client_data;
1153 
1154   // Initialize crypto handshake context with client IP name
1155   char crypto_name[96];
1156   SAFE_SNPRINTF(crypto_name, sizeof(crypto_name), "crypto_discovery_websocket_%s", client_ip);
1157   asciichat_error_t hs_init_result = crypto_handshake_init(crypto_name, &client_data->handshake_ctx, true);
1158   if (hs_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1159     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto handshake for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1160     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1161     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1162     return NULL;
1163   }
1164 
1165   // Set server identity keys for handshake
1166   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1167   client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1168   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_public_key.key, server->identity_public, 32);
1169   memcpy(client_data->handshake_ctx.server_private_key.key.ed25519, server->identity_secret, 64);
1170 
1171   // Generate synthetic socket ID for tcp_server registry
1172   static _Atomic int g_ws_synthetic_id = INT_MAX;
1173   int synthetic_id = atomic_fetch_sub(&g_ws_synthetic_id, 1);
1174   client_data->registry_id = synthetic_id;
1175 
1176   // Register in tcp_server with synthetic ID (so callbacks can find client_data)
1177   if (tcp_server_add_client(server->tcp_server, synthetic_id, client_data) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1178     log_error("Failed to register WebSocket client %s in registry", client_ip);
1179     SAFE_FREE(client_data);
1180     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1181     return NULL;
1182   }
1183 
1184   log_debug("WebSocket client %s registered (synthetic_id=%d, total=%zu)", client_ip, synthetic_id,
1185             tcp_server_get_client_count(server->tcp_server));
1186 
1187   // Determine if client authentication is required by checking server configuration
1188   bool auth_required = server->config.require_client_identity;
1189   ctx->auth_required = auth_required;
1190 
1191   // Skip crypto handshake only for wss:// connections WITHOUT authentication requirement
1192   // For plain ws://, always do crypto handshake
1193   // For wss:// WITH auth requirement, always do crypto handshake
1194   bool skip_handshake = ctx->is_secure && !auth_required;
1195 
1196   if (skip_handshake) {
1197     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1198     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - skipping crypto handshake (TLS encryption used, no auth required)",
1199              client_ip);
1200   } else {
1201     log_info("WebSocket connection from %s - proceeding with crypto handshake%s", client_ip,
1202              auth_required ? " (client authentication required)" : "");
1203 
1204     // Perform crypto handshake using shared server_like implementation
1205     log_debug("Performing crypto handshake with WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1206     asciichat_error_t hs_result = session_server_like_handshake(&client_data->handshake_ctx, transport);
1207     if (hs_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1208       log_warn("Crypto handshake failed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1209       tcp_server_remove_client(server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1210       SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1211       return NULL;
1212     }
1213 
1214     client_data->handshake_complete = true;
1215     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed for WebSocket client %s", client_ip);
1216   }
1217 
1218   // Main packet processing loop using transport recv
1219   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->tcp_server->running)) {
1220     void *recv_buffer = NULL;
1221     size_t recv_len = 0;
1222     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1223 
1224     asciichat_error_t recv_result = acip_transport_recv(transport, &recv_buffer, &recv_len, &alloc_buffer);
1225     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1226       // Check if this is a timeout or disconnect
1227       if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1228         log_debug("WebSocket client %s: receive timeout, continuing to wait for packets", client_ip);
1229         if (alloc_buffer) {
1230           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1231         }
1232         continue;
1233       }
1234 
1235       // Actual disconnect or fatal error
1236       log_info("WebSocket client %s disconnected", client_ip);
1237       if (alloc_buffer) {
1238         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1239       }
1240       break;
1241     }
1242 
1243     // Parse packet header from received data
1244     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t)) {
1245       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: received packet too small (%zu bytes)", client_ip, recv_len);
1246       if (alloc_buffer) {
1247         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1248       }
1249       continue;
1250     }
1251 
1252     const packet_header_t *header = (const packet_header_t *)recv_buffer;
1253     packet_type_t packet_type = (packet_type_t)NET_TO_HOST_U16(header->type);
1254     size_t payload_size = NET_TO_HOST_U32(header->length);
1255     const void *payload = (const uint8_t *)recv_buffer + sizeof(packet_header_t);
1256 
1257     // Validate payload size
1258     if (recv_len < sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size) {
1259       log_warn("WebSocket client %s: packet size mismatch (expected %zu, got %zu)", client_ip,
1260                sizeof(packet_header_t) + payload_size, recv_len);
1261       if (alloc_buffer) {
1262         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1263       }
1264       continue;
1265     }
1266 
1267     log_debug("Received packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s, length=%zu", packet_type, client_ip, payload_size);
1268 
1269     // Multi-key session creation protocol: block non-PING/PONG/SESSION_CREATE messages
1270     if (client_data->in_multikey_session_create) {
1271       bool allowed = (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE || packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PING ||
1272                       packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_PONG);
1273 
1274       if (!allowed) {
1275         log_warn("WebSocket client %s sent packet type 0x%02X during multi-key session creation - only "
1276                  "SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed",
1277                  client_ip, packet_type);
1278         acip_send_error(transport, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
1279                         "Only SESSION_CREATE/PING/PONG allowed during multi-key session creation");
1280         if (alloc_buffer) {
1281           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1282         }
1283         continue;
1284       }
1285     }
1286 
1287     // O(1) ACIP array-based dispatch
1288     acip_acds_callbacks_t callbacks = g_acds_callbacks;
1289     callbacks.app_ctx = server;
1290 
1291     asciichat_error_t dispatch_result =
1292         acip_handle_acds_packet(transport, packet_type, payload, payload_size, client_ip, &callbacks);
1293 
1294     if (dispatch_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1295       log_warn("ACIP handler failed for packet type 0x%02X from WebSocket client %s: %s", packet_type, client_ip,
1296                asciichat_error_string(dispatch_result));
1297     }
1298 
1299     if (alloc_buffer) {
1300       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1301     }
1302   }
1303 
1304   // Cleanup
1305   tcp_server_remove_client(server->tcp_server, synthetic_id);
1306   log_debug("WebSocket client %s unregistered (total=%zu)", client_ip,
1307             tcp_server_get_client_count(server->tcp_server));
1308 
1309   // Transport is owned by WebSocket server, don't destroy it here
1310 
1311   log_info("WebSocket client handler finished for %s", client_ip);
1312 
1313   SAFE_FREE(ctx);
1314 
1315   return NULL;
1316 }






References acip_handle_acds_packet(),
    acip_send_error(), acip_acds_callbacks_t::app_ctx,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_client_context_t::auth_required, buffer_pool_free(),
    websocket_client_context_t::client_ip, acds_server_t::config,
    crypto_handshake_init(), private_key_t::ed25519,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_complete,
    acds_client_data_t::handshake_ctx,
    acds_server_t::identity_public,
    acds_server_t::identity_secret,
    acds_client_data_t::in_multikey_session_create,
    websocket_client_context_t::is_secure,
    acds_client_data_t::joined_session, private_key_t::key,
    public_key_t::key, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    packet_header_t::length, log_debug, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NET_TO_HOST_U32, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_SESSION_CREATE,
    PACKET_TYPE_PING, PACKET_TYPE_PONG,
    acds_client_data_t::registry_id,
    acds_config_t::require_client_identity, tcp_server::running,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_SNPRINTF,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_public_key,
    session_server_like_handshake(), acds_server_t::tcp_server,
    tcp_server_add_client(), tcp_server_get_client_count(),
    tcp_server_remove_client(), acds_client_data_t::transport,
    websocket_client_context_t::transport, packet_header_t::type,
    private_key_t::type, public_key_t::type,
    acip_transport::user_data, and
    websocket_client_context_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main().
